kept.text:
In January 2019, there was a bug on Roblox where users with Builders Club and Outrageous Builders Club had their membership icons changed to Turbo Builders Club.

It was only visual and did not give users any Turbo perks.

This caused confusion and some outrage in the community, as OBC members thought they had been downgraded, while BC members believed they had been upgraded.

InceptionTime later clarified on Discord that it was a glitch. The bug lasted a few hours before being fixed.

kept.text:
the AMOUNT of kids about to become millionaires off gta 6 is actually crazy.

rockstar acquired fivem in 2023.

launched a paid creator marketplace in january 2026.
script bundles selling at $389.99.

developers averaging €5,000/month within 90 days.
roblox paid $1.5 billion to creators last year.

gta 6's players are older, richer, and already spent $8.6 billion inside a rockstar game.

the creator cut of that $8.6 billion was zero.

because the economy didn't exist yet.

it does now.

while you're out here waiting for launch day, 19 year olds are already running whitelist servers at $4,000/month recurring.

launching content rewards campaigns at $0.65 cpm.
filling 200-person waitlists for $1,300.

less than one month of server revenue to fill the list that pays for years.

kept.text:
Roblox is dropping a massive new barrier for creators starting May 19.

To publish a game to All Ages (including the new Roblox Kids & Select accounts for under 16), you now need:
>ID verification
>2FA enabled
>an active Roblox Plus subscription
>AND pass a brand new per-game evaluation process.

They say it’s for “safety”… but this is the same safety push that’s now slapping a monthly paywall and government ID requirement on creators just to reach kids. 

Small devs, hobbyists, and young creators are getting locked out. 

Privacy risks, paywalls, and slower discovery = fewer games and less growth for everyone.

Am i dumb for wanting to buy this with size n sit on it for a few years? 

Feel like $RBLX as a company just has such a larger user base, that at some point... the data alone is worth more than anything else. 

It is at a 33B marketcap, which ya thats concerning.
But dog wif hat was 1/11th of it, and all we did was get a picture... of a dog in a hat... 

They've beat earnings the last 3 quarters. 
People are spending more time online.

They are conscious of the userbase being younger and trying to tailor to an older audience. 

Idk, prolly gonna start to nibble on shares.

tweet.text:
That logic applies to literally everything online though

If ‘the dev could get compromised’ = don’t use it, then you shouldn’t use apps, websites, or even Roblox itself

because it’s not entirely ‘trust me bro’, it’s informed trust, like what permissions an extension asks for are visible

sure, there may be risk but the real issue isn’t extensions it’s random installs, users not checking perms, with a mix of social engineering (like how I described in my reply with frontend injections)
